author: Gabriel Vasile
message:
fuzz: improvements for exploring more code paths (#824)

* actions: select fuzz targets automatically

So that we don't need to edit the actions yaml each time a new fuzz
function is added.

* fuzz: help fuzzers explore more paths

1. make charset.FromHTML explore BOM codepaths too
2. make json fuzz explore query paths too
3. make FuzzMimetype explore truncated and full inputs too (some
   functions have logic depending on truncation)

author: Gabriel Vasile
message:
scan: fix panic and fuzz flags (#823)

Flags used for fuzzing were constant due to a logic error. That in turn,
prevented fuzzers from actually fuzzing. This commit fixes the fuzz
flags and the panic.

author: Gabriel Vasile
message:
charset: stop checking for ascii if utf8 is satisfied (#768)

Since ascii is a subset of utf8, we don't need to iterate over input
bytes twice. Just checking once that the content if valid utf8 is
enough.

FromPlain-4   145.15n ± 2%   36.52n ± 6%  -74.84% (p=0.002 n=6)

author: Gabriel Vasile
message: docs: mention String() can return different values (#820)

author: Gabriel Vasile
message:
mkv: use IANA registered media type video/matroska (#819)

As of 2024, the registered media type for mkv is video/matroska. https://www.iana.org/assignments/media-types/video/matroska

author: zhel1

author: Gabriel Vasile
message: rar: use vnd.rar instead of unregistered name (#809)

author: Gabriel Vasile
message:
all: increase defaultLimit from 3072 to 4096 (#796)

4096 matches bufio.defaultBufSize and the default page size on most
OSes.

author: Gabriel Vasile
message:
fuzz: cache interesting inputs between runs (#805)

* fuzz: cache interesting inputs between runs
* fuzz: reduce time because cache should improve coverage

author: Gabriel Vasile
message:
ndjson: fix edge-case with new-lines (#803)

When a json object was truncated and spread over multiple lines,
such that first line is an truncated JSON value and second one is a
valid JSON value it would be reported as NdJSON. This commits makes it
return text/plain.

author: Gabriel Vasile
message:
csv: drop DropLastLine for better performance (#799)

When parsing input that might have been truncated, instead of dropping the last
line we will allow it to have any number of fields as long as it's less
fields that the header and the input was consumed entirely.
That should speed up detection because we stop iterating over bytes from
the tail and only CSV parse what we're interested in.
Changing NewParser to receive a pointer of scan.Bytes is done so we can
check if those bytes have been fully consumed from outside the parser.

author: Gabriel Vasile
message:
MP3: add a frame parser and do linear search for headers (#778)

* mp3: add a parser for Layer3 frames

It is needed because mp3 decoders are supposed to do linear searches for
frames. Fixes #775 and #310.

author: Gabriel Vasile
message:
actions: restrict id-token permission to the job that needs it (#788)

This was found by pedantic zizmor. The change doesn't do anything since there is only one job in the workflow, but just in case a new one is added.
Additionally, restrict concurrent runs of the benchmark job on the same PR. The benchmark job takes quite a lot of time.

author: Gabriel Vasile
message: security: threat model and incident response plan (#786)

author: Gabriel Vasile
message: codecov: use OIDC instead of long-lived token (#785)

author: Gabriel Vasile
message:
actions: don't persist credentials on checkout

Issue found by zimzor. Explained in https://github.com/actions/checkout/issues/485

author: Gabriel Vasile
message:
mp3: harden ID3v2 check

Previous check was prone to false positives because it only checked the
first three chars being "ID3". Current implementation checks the
version, flags and size bits.

author: Gabriel Vasile
message: actions: pin go tools to commits too

author: Gabriel Vasile
message:
actions: pin to commit instead of tag

Tags are mutable. Be it an action gets compromised, a tag can be
updated.

author: Gabriel Vasile
message:
